summary:
MEGAN'S DIAGNOSIS AND EARLY STRUGGLES: Brandon shares the history of his daughter, Megan, who developed severe special needs after receiving a flu shot and the MMR vaccine at 18 months old. Prior to the vaccinations, Megan was developing normally, speaking in full sentences, and had normal Apgar scores. Shortly after the shots, she experienced a high fever, lost her ability to speak, and entered a state that Brandon describes as a bubble. She suffered from severe bowel impaction, holding her bowels for weeks, which caused intense swelling. Her behavior became highly destructive, including violent tantrums, biting herself, wiping feces on walls and furniture, and staying awake almost all night, which caused severe fatigue for Brandon and his wife, Diana.

MEDICAL PROGNOSIS AND ALTERNATIVE TREATMENTS: Medical professionals informed Brandon and Diana that Megan would never mentally progress beyond the age of two, suggesting they surrender her to the state. Rejecting this advice, Brandon and Diana sought various treatments to help their daughter. They pursued Applied Behavior Analysis, chelation therapy to remove heavy metals, DNA testing, magnet testing, and consulted Defeat Autism Now practitioners. The financial burden of these treatments, coupled with a lack of government assistance, eventually forced the family into bankruptcy, yet none of these medical interventions improved Megan's condition.

MINISTRY CALLING AND CHURCH EXPERIENCES: Brandon recounts receiving a calling from the Lord to start a healing ministry and a YouTube channel, which he initially resisted because he felt hypocritical ministering to others while his own daughter remained unhealed. Seeking divine intervention, the family attended several healing services. During an Andrew Wommack service, Megan caused a disruption by turning off the auditorium lights. They also visited a ministry in Dallas that charged them money for an attempted exorcism. Brandon describes attending a service in Crescent, Oklahoma, where a Romanian missionary named Ronald prayed for him, delivering him from intense suicidal thoughts and depression.

DIVINE VISITATION AND INITIAL BREAKTHROUGHS: On October 29th, Brandon experienced a tangible visitation from Jesus in his kitchen. Jesus touched Brandon, stated that his prayers had been heard, and promised that Megan would begin to amend. Although Diana did not see Jesus, she physically felt the presence in the room. By the following Monday, Megan's teacher reported that she had parroted over 50 words, making eye contact and using vocabulary for the first time since her regression.

HEALING PROGRESS AT BILLY BURKE MEETINGS: Brandon details their ongoing journey at Billy Burke healing services. During their first visit, they sat in the back of a crowded auditorium due to Megan's behavioral meltdowns. Billy Burke called them to the front and prayed for Megan, who was 13 at the time. Within 24 hours of this prayer, Megan had her first independent bowel movement and was completely potty trained within a couple of weeks. Her behavior improved significantly, allowing the family to attend church and visit public places again. Brandon maintains that Megan is the healed resisting autism and states that she continues to make progress, expanding her vocabulary and daily functioning.
rawData: [May 08, 2025]-[Answering some questions about my child with special needs]-[https://youtu.be/5PrG73v7c3E]-[Hey, good morning family. Praise the Lord. Thank you for joining me today. I'm going to talk about something pretty hard, but many of you don't know about my daughter and her testimony and the story of what she's been through. I thought I'd talk about it because there are a lot of new faces. Many of you do know, but many of you do not. For the ones who don't know, I'm going to talk about it because people ask, "What's the noise in the background when you do your videos?" They email asking what that noise is. They think it's a devil or something. That's my daughter, and she has special needs. When she was 18 months old, she got the flu shot. It's very hard to talk about, but I talk about it because one day I believe it's going to be the greatest testimony the world has ever seen. That's what I just keep saying to myself. She got the flu shot and was developing normally. Her Apgar scores were completely normal when she was born. Everything was completely fine. She was talking and making full sentences, like any two-year-old would do. She was starting to really develop and everything was okay. When we went in, her doctor suggested the flu was really bad that year and said every parent should have this done. As young adults, we were fearful of that, so I told the lady to go ahead and give her the flu shot. We got her the flu shot at the same time we gave her her measles,, and rubella shot, her MMR. Within just a couple of days, she ran a very high fever. All of a sudden, it's like she went from being a happy little girl developing normally to a child completely in a bubble. She lost all speech and just made weird humming noises. She went into a complete zombie state. It was so bad that I called the doctor and said, "Something is really wrong with our child." My wife called and we demanded they see her. They said, "Oh, she's just having a little reaction to the flu vaccination and running a fever. It's okay. This will pass. Don't worry about it." I said, "No, there's something much more wrong. There's something really wrong." Megan got worse and worse. Her bowels stopped working. She wouldn't go to the bathroom anymore. Her belly would swell up to the size of a beach ball. As a child, she would hold her bowels for three weeks at a time without going and would cross her legs because of the sensation of going. It was so bad that she would swell up huge, and she'd be so impacted they thought they would have to perform emergency surgery on her because of the X-rays. It was absolute hell on earth. She started having the worst tantrums and biting herself. She would wipe feces all down our hall all day long with what she would dig out of her hiney. It was nasty. She would wipe it on furniture, her bed, and our walls. Every day I'd come home from work and Diana would be home with her, just weeping because of how bad the day was. She would take everything in the bathroom and put it in the kitchen and everything in the kitchen into the bathroom. She put things from the bedroom into the living room. Every year when we put up our Christmas tree, she would take all the ornaments and lights down. We'd go to bed at night, and then she would put them in a drawer in the kitchen. This went on for years. The severity is such that I can't even tell you with words how bad it truly was. She would get two hours of sleep at night, keeping us up all night long. Diana and I were walking zombies because of how tired we were. I would go to work and pass out in the hall because of how tired I was. When I was working on a Saturday cleaning the church, I would go into the men's stall and just lay my head over because I was too tired to function, even while driving. It was so bad. We would drive her around at night in the car just trying to get her to sleep. We had a diesel car and the vibration and loudness would help her fall asleep as we were driving. But the moment we stopped the car and tried to take her to her bed, she'd wake up. There was nothing that kept her asleep. I would sit in the house and rock her for hours as she would try to fall asleep, listening to Christian music to the point where if I hear those songs now, it bothers me. I can't listen to that music anymore because of how traumatizing those moments were. I know many of you have children with special needs or autism, and my heart goes out to you. I pray for you all the time. People send me letters. I remember those days when we didn't think she would be where she's at right now. I prayed and believed, but the doctors all told us she would never progress beyond the mental capacity of a two-year-old. He said, "She'll never talk. She'll never be potty trained. She'll never have a normal functioning life. You're going to be this way forever. Why don't you sign her over to the state and make her an orphan so you can start over and have a normal life?" I told him I'll never do that. I will raise this little girl and she'll be healed. He said we were in denial, in La-La Land. He said, "I have over 3,000 documented diagnoses since I've started my practice, and I've never one time seen a child like this get any better or have a normal life." He said, "I'm telling you all, you are in denial," and basically he was rebuking us, telling us that we needed a reality check. Diana said, "Sir, you may have never had a child come out of this as severe as this, but we are going to be the first ones. We're going to be the ones who come back and tell you that Jesus Christ is a healer and she's going to come out of this." We're still believing that after 20 years. We're standing on it and believing for the full manifestation. I want you to know that even someone who has the relationship that I have with the Lord goes through things. Not everything is always roses. I think it's interesting because we watch other people on Facebook comment about what their child's going through, but I would never make public many things that I went through when Megan was first diagnosed. We didn't even say the word autism. I wouldn't even let you say it in my presence because I'm not claiming it. I don't have that. I'm resisting what the devil has tried to put on us. I refuse to acknowledge it and say, "Listen, this is what we have." No, I am resisting. I am resisting it. I am not claiming it. I am denying its right to be here. And by claiming it and saying, "I have this," is not what we have. I am the healed resisting autism. I am the healed. She is the healed resisting autism. Whenever she makes those noises and those hums and all the things that she does, we don't even notice it anymore. We're so used to it because of how many years it's been going on—for 20 years. You just don't even think about it. But whenever somebody's watching your video, they're thinking, "What is that?" So I try to explain to y'all a little bit what we're going through. The Lord told me, "Brandon, I've called you to the healing ministry. I've called you to go into all the world and lay hands on the sick and they'll recover." I said, "How am I going to do that? Whenever I pray for my little girl and I haven't seen the full manifestation, I'll be a hypocrite. If I've got a child that's overcoming this and we don't see the full manifestation, how am I supposed to go and pray for the sick and then tell them that Jesus is a healer, that you're a healer, whenever I don't even see my daughter's full manifestation?" I argued with him and he said, "Brandon, your daughter does not validate my word. My word validates itself. It's a pillar by itself. I don't need Megan's full manifestation to prove my word is true. My word is true and it stands alone. Regardless of your circumstances, just be obedient and do what I've called you to do." I wrestled with this over and over again until he kept coming to me and said, "I want you to start a YouTube channel and do these things." I wrestled with it for two years, pulling videos and not putting them on YouTube. For two years I did this before anybody would ever really look at it. I put a video up for a couple days and took it down because I felt so inadequate, so tormented by it. And the Lord told me, "I want you to do what I've called you to do." Well, we went to a Billy Burke meeting—I always talk about Billy Burke. It was my birthday, and I went for a birthday present for me. I wanted to go see Billy Burke, and I was excited about it. We got there and had to sit at the very back because Megan was acting so bad. Before this, we went to Andrew Wommack to get him to pray for her, and she was in the back of the service in this hotel room in Norman, Oklahoma. There was a huge light switchboard on the back of the auditorium, and Megan decided she was going to throw a fit and push those buttons. In the middle of Andrew Wommack preaching his sermon, all the lights went out in the whole auditorium and it went completely black. There were probably 3,000 people in there or more, and it was complete—you couldn't see your hand in front of your face inside this auditorium with the doors closed. Andrew was on the front stage and he goes, "Hello?" You know how he's so real country. I literally thought I could crawl underneath the table and bury myself. That's how embarrassed I was. I was freaking out. I grabbed a hold of her and I said, "I'm so sorry. I'm so sorry." It was in the middle of the thing and he's preaching and everybody's looking. He turned around looking at us like, "What happened?" When the guys came over with flashlights looking to try to turn on the lights again—they were renting the building and didn't know how to turn the lights on, but they didn't lock them. Megan did that. We were looking for prayer. I think he prayed for us, but nothing really happened. Nothing against him; nothing happened. Just being real. After it was all over, I walked away feeling so defeated because I knew that I was expecting something to happen at Andrew Wommack's service. We took her to that, and that was one of the only services we took her to for a long time because then we had people telling us that she had a devil. I told you all about that. I took her down to a ministry in the Dallas area, and they charged us $500 to try to cast the devils out of her. I'm not going to get into that. Then we took her to a Billy Burke service. When we went to the Billy Burke service, we sat at the very back. We were at a massive church, a huge church. There were so many people you couldn't stir them with a stick. I was so disappointed because I wanted her to be down at the front and get prayed for, but so did everybody else that has needs. The lines were long for prayer and Megan was losing it. She was having one of her biggest meltdowns and she was pushing against me, freaking out. I carried her up to the front holding her, and she was resisting me and pushing off of me. He says, "Everybody who needs a miracle, get down to the very front. I'm going to pray for you. I'm closing the service." I was so disappointed because there were people all around me that had a lot more issues, it seemed, than what we had—broken legs and all these things going on with their bodies—and they were looking for healing, too. But there were just too many people to pray for. All of a sudden, my wife comes over and stands next to me with my other two, and Braden was filming it like he always does. He films everything. Billy Burke, by the spirit of God, looks over at us and he says, "You two come here." He calls us over for us to pray for him. And you'll see the video right here. "Is this your daughter?" "Yes, sir. Bring her over here. She was making all the noise over there, right? What's going on with your daughter?" "She got a flu vaccination. It caused autism." "How old is she now?" "She's 13." So that's the video. That was the day that Megan started—the day that Billy Burke prayed for our little girl and she started having bowel functions for the first time. She came home and for the first time had a bowel movement by herself and was able to go on the stool and was completely potty trained within a couple weeks. It happened within 24 hours, but it was after he prayed for her. Within a couple weeks, God started to restore Megan. We were able to start going to church without her freaking out after that visitation from the Lord. We weren't able to go into public places—we were not able to go eat in public places or go to church or go to Walmart or go to any of these places because Megan would have such a meltdown. We would try to put her in the buggy and as she got older, I'd put her in a wheelchair, to the point where I just sat out in the car because it was so rough. We have videos—we took her to stores; we've done it now to prove that we've taken her into places because we want to document it. When the full manifestation comes, God alone, with all the documents, will get all the glory for what he's done for Megan. One day I was praying and our staff at our church was having a harvest party—that's what we want to call it because we don't celebrate Halloween—but they were having a get-together at the pastor's house. Everybody was going over there for barbecue and refreshments, things like that. We couldn't go because we didn't have childcare for Megan because nobody really could watch Megan because of how bad she was. Megan would not act right. It was awful. So we never really left her with anybody because we knew that they couldn't handle it and we didn't want to hear it: "Megan was this, Megan was that." Oh, I don't care. I live it. You know, keep your comments to yourself. So family, even family, I couldn't deal with it. Don't want to deal with it. "Megan wet in our chair. Megan peed or had a pee accident in our chair or ruined it." I don't want to hear it. You know, I live it. She's destroyed three cars. I mean, seriously, that's how bad it was. So when people don't relate and they can't handle something, they attack it or they accuse or say, "Hey, this is what you should be doing." And when you're doing all you can do as a father with a special needs child, I don't need any correction. You know, I need sympathy and I need you to love on me whenever you are down and you're hurt and bad. You get that? And a lot of people always feel like they have their suggestion of what they think you should be doing because they have it all figured out. They look at your life through their little magnifying glass going, "I see that you have a flaw here," and they have no idea because they've never been through anything in their whole entire life, but they have a suggestion and a cure for you. Does that make sense? But everybody who's never been through anything knows how to fix your situation until you've lived it for less than a 24-hour period. And even the people who have—this is interesting—the people who have even more of an opinion just encounter you and they think they can fix all your issues. But if you lived it for a week, you'd want to hurt yourself physically. You know what I'm saying? It was so bad. I wanted to run my car off a cliff so many days because I could not function. And that's how bad it was. Sleep? I wanted to run off a bridge. I wanted to self-harm. That's how bad it was. I spiritually had to grab a hold of it. And the reason I'm still here is because I had that will inside of myself by the Holy Spirit to say, "You can do this. You be a father to that child and you be a husband to that wife and you don't abandon them by being a quitter." But there were so many days that I went to an evangelist. I had such a deep root of depression on me and I was so suicidal that the evangelist who was at the front of the church saw it on me. He called me up to the front and said, "Sir, you need to come up here. There's a devil of suicide trying to come against you and I'm going to pray for you right now. Jesus is going to set you free." I was so broken at that time. I literally thought I was lost. I didn't think I was going to make it out of it. And I remember sitting in the back of that church just looking for something. I left early that morning thinking, "I'm not going to go home today." Diana was at home with Megan, and I just thought, "I need help. I need help." And I drove to this little town, Crescent, Oklahoma. And this man named Ronald was there. Ronald was there and he was preaching. And we had our son at this time; he was just a little baby. And I was so down with depression and suicide that I thought there's nothing else I can do. I just got to quit. And I went to the back of this church just looking for hope. And he called me out and he said, "Come down to the front." And he laid hands on me. And I'm telling you, that spirit came off of me and broke off of me. And the power of God set me free. It changed me forever. Yeah, the spirit tried to come back, but I resisted it more and more because I knew what it was like to be free. And there's so many times when you get to that place of desperation that you don't feel there's a way out of the hole that you're in and you think the only way out is death. But I'm trying to tell you today that Jesus has a plan for your life. And if I would have done what I was thinking I was going to do back then, I wouldn't be here with my son right now preaching the gospel to you. There's a place of desperation. There's a place of darkness that some people will never know until you've been there. A place of hurt, a place of discouragement that is so deep and so vast that you don't know how you're ever going to fill that bubble because you think there's no way out. And I'm so thankful for Ronald. He was a missionary from Romania and he came over there and he prayed for me. He gave me a prophetic word and told me there's people that have abandoned you. And he gave me this word and it was dead smack right on. After he prayed for us, then I went and I got Diana and I said, "Oh my God, baby, I have hope. I have hope. This man called me out. You know the circumstance." She didn't know how bad I was because I try to be strong all the time for her. And I went over and I grabbed her and I got the kids and I took her to this revival that night. He was at a Pizza Hut eating after church, and I took her over there and I said, "Let me just try to sow a seed." Because I knew the power of a seed. It was maybe like 20 bucks; I don't know because we didn't have nothing. But I told her, "Let's just sow that in faith, believing for breakthrough in our household. Let's believe for what seemed impossible that God's going to turn it around." I go over this story and I cry every time and it's still painful. So Diana got prayed for, Megan got prayed for, we all went home. But I was so broken by it. So now I go back to the thing where I told you we were at the party, and this happened right after that. That's what I'm trying to tell y'all. This happened right after that. So, it was October the 29th and I was standing at the sink doing dishes and I had just come home from work and I said, "Lord, do you hear any of my prayers? Have you abandoned me, Lord? Do you care that I'm going through any of this? Do you see the pain?" Just fussing. "Do you see the pain that I'm going through?" Just scrubbing the dishes. Diana's in there dealing with Megan and all of a sudden Jesus comes in. And this is the first time I can tangibly tell you he was not a vision; he was tangible, tangible like this. He shows up in the kitchen. The presence of God felt so much. I turned around as I'm fussing and Jesus said, "I've heard all of your prayers." And I turned around and I melted on the ground because I could not stand in front of his presence. And he looked at me and he bent over—I can remember his face like it was yesterday. He laid his hand upon my shoulder and he told me, "Brandon, I've heard all of your prayers." He said, "What do you have need of, son?" I said, "Lord, don't you know?" I said, "Megan." And he said, "According to your faith, Brandon, be it done unto you." I said, "Lord, I want Megan to be completely healed." And he said, "She will start to amend from this." And he ministered to me some things that I won't talk about, but he loved on me. It was private stuff. And he laid his hand on my hand and he blessed me. Diana comes around the corner and she doesn't see him, but she feels the presence of the Lord in the house. It was so strong and so powerful that it made her stand, holding to the wall. I remember her coming in and she said, "Oh my god." She goes, "Are you dying?" because she thought I was going home because I was on the ground, heaped over, just weeping. And I was sobbing because the presence of the Lord so filled my house with the glory of the Lord when Jesus showed up. He touched me. He looks over and he smiles at her. He didn't say nothing. And she doesn't see it, but she feels the presence of the Lord. I said, "Baby, Jesus is here. Jesus is here." She says, "I can sense. I can feel, but I don't see it. I don't see him." And she stood there and she started weeping at the presence of the Lord. And the Lord told us that Megan was going to get better at that time. To make a long story short, I know there's some of this I'm never going to tell publicly. He blessed me and he talked about the ministry. That's all you need to know. Then he said to me, "Brandon, my father's calling me. I must go." He turned around and, just as he came, he disappeared right out of my kitchen. I wept. I look over at Megan expecting her to be better, and nothing. You know the Bible says as they went they were healed; it wasn't instantaneous that Jesus healed some people, it was as they went. That was Saturday. So Monday she went to school and her teacher called me in the middle of the afternoon. She said, "Brandon, what did you do to Megan? Something's happened." And I said, "Hold on, Kimberly. What do you mean? What did I do to Megan? What do you mean what happened?" and she said, "Explain to me how she's saying words." She said over 50 parroted words today. We're talking a child that had no vocabulary at all, was not able to function at all, able to keep eye contact for the first time, and was repeating. She made a list of all the words that Megan was saying on a piece of paper and we still have that today. We still have it today because it was a miracle. Megan still says things like parroting things all the time now even, and she says, "I love you." She says, "Hi Daddy." She says, "Hi Mommy." Those are the things Megan does now. She's starting, and it's becoming more and more every day. "I want food. I want water." Things like that, from nothing at all to now starting to say little phrases. Yeah, it's been a long time, but we've stood. And Megan, we were so wanting to help her. We do ABA and do all this stuff. We thought we put it all on our credit cards. We did everything we could to try to help our child to get out of that because there was no government assistance. There was nothing really for Megan. They would only allot so much a month. So we just kept charging. We kept doing things. We had to file bankruptcy because we were trying to find her help. There were all these different doctors saying all these different things saying, "Hey, if you do this, this is going to work. If you do chelation, whatever, they run the IV and they do all the things through your body to pull the heavy metals out from the shots, doing enemas, all kinds of things with chelation or whatever it's called." Sorry, I butchered the word. We did all of it. DAN! doctors, you name it. We spent thousands. DNA testing, magnet testing, all kinds of things over the years. It's kind of like the woman with the issue of blood whenever she said she spent all she had but she grew worse. That's the way it felt like with us. All of these things didn't help but Jesus. We went to a Billy Burke meeting and you saw what happened and the power of God came on Megan and that's why we're hooked to going to Billy Burke. Because we went and spent... we went to all the big healing evangelists. I'm not here to throw any of them under the bus because it's not their faith that does anything. I was seeking the anointing. But in a place of desperation, it was so bad that I didn't think we were even going to be able to stay. God pointed us out. God through him: "Hey, you two. Come over here." You watched it. It was just God. There were hundreds of people, if not a thousand people up at the front or more, and God did that. And that was the beginning of our journey. Then we went to Eufaula, and now Megan's getting better and better and better. And we went to the thing in Fort Lauderdale. So when you say, "Brandon, why are you so addicted to going to Billy Burke?" because it's the first sign of hope. It was the first sign of true hope other than what I had whenever she was with that visitation from Jesus. That was a ten-year window. Almost ten years, whatever it was. It was a long time. Plus ten years before we saw any other things starting to get better. Then we went to a Billy Burke meeting and then it was like, boom! And now it's starting to roll. But I'm saying it's like what he always says: "God doesn't dangle a carrot." Brandon, God doesn't dangle a carrot. He doesn't heal you partway without getting the full manifestation to dangle a carrot and tell you, "Well, I'm going to do part of it and not do the whole thing." I believe Megan is a work in progress. That's where that all came out of. "I'm a work in progress." We talk about it all the time. God, we're a work in progress. Regardless if we miss it, if we fall, if we stumble, whatever, we're a work in progress with life. And I believe there's a lot of you that are watching this video today that can relate some way. Maybe not all the way. Maybe your circumstances are a lot worse. Maybe your circumstance is you've been given a death sentence. But I'm telling you, it's the faith that keeps on keeping on. Whenever it doesn't look like you're going to get out of it, whenever it looks the darkest, is whenever you get that bulldog inside of you saying, "I'm not giving up." It's like when I went to that Ronald meeting; it was like, "I need help." When you come to your end: "I need help. I can't do this anymore." And I know there's a lot of you that are in that place right now that say, "I need help. I can't do this anymore." I had an email or something, somebody saying, "My husband's dealing with this and he's ready to hurt himself because of the fact that he has tinnitus in his ears." And that's where this came out of after I did this video for people like that. Because I'm telling you, whenever you go through something and it seems that it's never going to stop and you think, "I'm just going to do myself in. I can't do this anymore." And there's people that have all these things that go through and you think the only way out is death. The only way out of this circumstance is to quit. I'm trying to tell you as a living testimony that there's more. And God has a plan for your life. It's not for you to hurt yourself or do something silly that's forever permanent. Don't do something permanent for a temporary problem. This too shall pass. Those dark days will pass. I believe that God is a rewarder of those that diligently seek Him. And whenever it seems the darkest, light's just around the corner. I believe that your circumstances are going to change today. I'm here to tell you that I'm still walking through some of the valley. I'm still walking through the times of hardship. I'm still walking through the things that don't look in the natural that I tell you I deal with with Megan. We had a really rough day with her on Sunday. I didn't even go to church. But is it all falling? The world's falling in. Oh, no. I thought you were getting better. No, devil, you ain't stealing my victory. Get off! We're going to church. We're going to be a family. We're going to do these things. Do you understand? Even whenever the circumstance tries to change and it throws up in your face to try to get you down, to try to suck your joy out, that's whenever you stand the most. Whenever the waves and the storm winds blow, that's when you stand the most. And I'm here to tell you, sometimes it gets darker before it gets light. Sometimes the valley of the shadow of death feels like it's going to devour you. But He said He would never leave you, and He said He would never forsake you. Lo, and to the end of the earth, He said He would be with you always. Though I walk through the valley of the shadow of death, I will fear no evil because Thy rod and Thy staff, they comfort me. And that's why I say, my God has a big staff. My God has a big rod, and He's going to bust you in the head, devil. I'm not going to allow you to get me down and ever do that again. It's what you think on. What you think on the most becomes bigger in your life. Sometimes whenever all you see in front of your face is the problem, it's hard to see anything else. I get it. Whenever it's so close to your nose—your nose is in it and it's like poo and somebody's rubbing that in your face—the devil's rubbing in your face that you're never going to get out of this. 'You're going to be in this hole forever,' and you're going to go, 'I can't breathe anymore.' I get it. But God! And I'm just here to tell you a little glimpse of hope and to tell you a little bit about my past to encourage you that you can keep on keeping on too. You can keep on keeping on. Yeah, sometimes we walk through it and we're crying. Sometimes we're walking and crying. I'm telling you, there are days I've cried. I cried today. Look at me. It's painful.
